title: Documentation: Today Brief History
description: Update relevant documentation to reflect the refactored TodayView (now a brief list), the new BriefDetailView and BriefCardView components, and the getBriefs APIClient method. Clearly note the graceful-degradation contract: the tab shows today-only until the backend ships GET /briefs, at which point full history appears automatically without an app change.
acceptance_criteria: getBriefs() method and its fallback to getTodayBrief() on 404 are described
Backend dependency on GET /briefs and the degradation behaviour are noted
TodayView's new list architecture is reflected accurately

handoff_summary.completed: docs/API.md updated with: (1) GET /briefs row in the Reads table, (2) curl examples for the briefs endpoints, (3) new "iOS client: Today tab" section covering all four acceptance criteria.
handoff_summary.remaining: Nothing — all acceptance criteria addressed in a single docs pass.
handoff_summary.risks: GET /briefs is not yet confirmed deployed on the backend; the doc calls this out explicitly (graceful degradation). If the endpoint shape differs from what is implemented in the iOS client, the doc may need a revision once the backend ships.
handoff_summary.next_action: No follow-up required. Documentation is complete and consistent with the implemented code.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — docs/API.md has no overlap with any sibling bead's file scope.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Placed iOS-specific component documentation inside docs/API.md (the only existing docs file) rather than creating a separate architecture doc, to keep the client reference self-contained. The new section "iOS client: Today tab" is clearly scoped to the iOS implementation so it does not pollute the generic API contract sections.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: BriefModelTests.swift covers Brief/BriefRef decoding, DateDisplay.mediumDate formatting, getBriefs URL construction, and PaginatedResponse<Brief> decoding — all behaviours referenced in the new documentation section are test-covered.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: GET /briefs backend endpoint may not yet be deployed; the graceful-degradation fallback is documented and the iOS app handles this at runtime, but the API.md entry for GET /briefs is forward-looking.

conflict_risks: None — docs/API.md has no overlap with any sibling bead's file scope.

execution_history.summary: Updated docs/API.md to document the refactored Today tab: added GET /briefs to the endpoint table and examples, and added a new "iOS client: Today tab" section covering TodayView's list architecture, BriefCardView, BriefDetailView, the getBriefs() APIClient method, and the graceful-degradation contract (falls back to GET /brief/today on 404).

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing any documentation, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform documentation; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — documentation agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
